headline: Sabah Sulu Claim Exposes 280,000 Undocumented Filipinos' Status
context: The 2024 Sulu heirs arbitration reignited questions about 280,000 undocumented Filipino residents in Sabah, many of whom have lived there for decades without legal status.

cards.big: 280,000 undocumented Filipinos live in Sabah — 8% of the state's population.
cards.sub: Many arrived during the 1970s Mindanao conflict. Their children and grandchildren remain stateless.

cards.big: Project IC in the 1990s allegedly granted citizenship to Filipino migrants.
cards.sub: A Royal Commission of Inquiry in 2014 confirmed irregularities but no criminal charges resulted.

cards.big: Stateless children in Sabah cannot access public education or healthcare.
cards.sub: An estimated 80,000 children are affected. NGOs provide parallel schooling for 12,000.

cards.big: Sabah's documented population grew 285% between 1970 and 2020.
cards.sub: No other Malaysian state experienced comparable growth. Immigration is the primary driver.

cards.big: 80,000 stateless children born in Malaysia. They did not choose to be undocumented. The state created their status.
Sabah's undocumented population needs a registration pathway that separates border security from humanitarian obligation. Children born in Malaysia should not be stateless.
